speaker: CHORUS
text: Now all the youth of England are on fire,

speaker: CHORUS
text: And silken dalliance in the wardrobe lies;

speaker: CHORUS
text: Now thrive the armorers, and honor’s thought

speaker: CHORUS
text: Reigns solely in the breast of every man.

speaker: CHORUS
text: They sell the pasture now to buy the horse,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Following the mirror of all Christian kings

speaker: CHORUS
text: With wingèd heels, as English Mercurys.

speaker: CHORUS
text: For now sits Expectation in the air

speaker: CHORUS
text: And hides a sword, from hilts unto the point,

speaker: CHORUS
text: With crowns imperial, crowns, and coronets

speaker: CHORUS
text: Promised to Harry and his followers.

speaker: CHORUS
text: The French, advised by good intelligence

speaker: CHORUS
text: Of this most dreadful preparation,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Shake in their fear, and with pale policy

speaker: CHORUS
text: Seek to divert the English purposes.

speaker: CHORUS
text: O England, model to thy inward greatness,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Like little body with a mighty heart,

speaker: CHORUS
text: What might’st thou do, that honor would thee do,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Were all thy children kind and natural!

speaker: CHORUS
text: But see, thy fault France hath in thee found out,

speaker: CHORUS
text: A nest of hollow bosoms, which he fills

speaker: CHORUS
text: With treacherous crowns, and three corrupted men—

speaker: CHORUS
text: One, Richard, Earl of Cambridge, and the second,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Henry, Lord Scroop of Masham, and the third,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Sir Thomas Grey, knight, of Northumberland—

speaker: CHORUS
text: Have, for the gilt of France (O guilt indeed!),

speaker: CHORUS
text: Confirmed conspiracy with fearful France,

speaker: CHORUS
text: And by their hands this grace of kings must die,

speaker: CHORUS
text: If hell and treason hold their promises,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Ere he take ship for France, and in Southampton.

speaker: CHORUS
text: Linger your patience on, and we’ll digest

speaker: CHORUS
text: Th’ abuse of distance, force a play.

speaker: CHORUS
text: The sum is paid, the traitors are agreed,

speaker: CHORUS
text: The King is set from London, and the scene

speaker: CHORUS
text: Is now transported, gentles, to Southampton.

speaker: CHORUS
text: There is the playhouse now, there must you sit,

speaker: CHORUS
text: And thence to France shall we convey you safe

speaker: CHORUS
text: And bring you back, charming the narrow seas

speaker: CHORUS
text: To give you gentle pass; for, if we may,

speaker: CHORUS
text: We’ll not offend one stomach with our play.

speaker: CHORUS
text: But, till the King come forth, and not till then,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Unto Southampton do we shift our scene.
